SEARCH
Package app.service.impl.anomalies

Class BaseAnomaliesDetectionService<T extends TimeSeriesRow>

java.lang.Object
app.service.impl.anomalies.BaseAnomaliesDetectionService<T>
Type Parameters:
T - The underlying time-series data row type under evaluation, extending TimeSeriesRow.
All Implemented Interfaces:
AnomaliesDetectionService<T>
Direct Known Subclasses:
BaseDataRecordAnomaliesDetectionService, BaseFinancialIndicatorsAnomaliesDetectionService
public abstract class BaseAnomaliesDetectionService<T extends TimeSeriesRow> extends Object implements AnomaliesDetectionService<T>
Concrete implementation of AnomaliesDetectionService that utilizes the Smile library's IsolationForest algorithm to detect structural deviations and anomalies within financial datasets.

This service tracks the historical training context data used for each initialized dto instance, maps multidimensional generic records into double primitives, evaluates anomaly scores against a percentage sensitivity ceiling, and calculates feature impact contributions to explain detected anomalies.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    protected abstract Map<String,Double>
    calculateContributions(smile.anomaly.IsolationForest isolationForest, double[] record, List<T> trainingData)
    Computes the algorithmic feature contribution percentage breakdown explaining how heavily each independent variable influenced a specific anomaly score.
    protected abstract double[]
    calculateMeans(List<T> data)
    Computes the mathematical mean values across all financial data dimensions from a collection of data records to form a centralized operational reference point.
    protected abstract double[][]
    parseData(List<T> data)
    Transforms a generic collection list of financial domain record objects into a primitive 2D matrix structure format compatible with the underlying mathematical library matrices.
    List<AnomalyResult<T>>
    searchForAnomaly(smile.anomaly.IsolationForest isolationForest, List<T> data, double threshold)
    Evaluates a collection of active target financial records against the specified pre-trained dto to identify elements exceeding the anomaly threshold.
    smile.anomaly.IsolationForest
    trainIsolationForest(List<T> data, int treesNumber)
    Fits and builds an Isolation Forest mathematical dto configured with an optimized tree ceiling profile based on the size of the incoming collection and the given number of trees.

  • Constructor Details

    • BaseAnomaliesDetectionService

      public BaseAnomaliesDetectionService()

  • Method Details

    • trainIsolationForest

      public smile.anomaly.IsolationForest trainIsolationForest(List<T> data, int treesNumber)
      Fits and builds an Isolation Forest mathematical dto configured with an optimized tree ceiling profile based on the size of the incoming collection and the given number of trees. Stores the initialized training context on completion.
      Specified by:
      trainIsolationForest in interface AnomaliesDetectionService<T extends TimeSeriesRow>
      Parameters:
      data - A List of historical generic time-series instances used to establish normal behavior bounds.
      treesNumber - The number of trees to build during the anomaly detection process.
      Returns:
      A fully trained, structurally optimized IsolationForest dto instance.

    • searchForAnomaly

      public List<AnomalyResult<T>> searchForAnomaly(smile.anomaly.IsolationForest isolationForest, List<T> data, double threshold)
      Evaluates a collection of active target financial records against the specified pre-trained dto to identify elements exceeding the anomaly threshold.
      Specified by:
      searchForAnomaly in interface AnomaliesDetectionService<T extends TimeSeriesRow>
      Parameters:
      isolationForest - The pre-trained IsolationForest engine used for scoring.
      data - The working List of target generic rows to scan for anomalies.
      threshold - The contamination sensitivity ceiling. Records with scores greater than this value are flagged.
      Returns:
      A List containing parsed AnomalyResult items detailing the feature contribution breakages for flagged records.

    • calculateContributions

      protected abstract Map<String,Double> calculateContributions(smile.anomaly.IsolationForest isolationForest, double[] record, List<T> trainingData)
      Computes the algorithmic feature contribution percentage breakdown explaining how heavily each independent variable influenced a specific anomaly score.

      Calculations evaluate spatial distance impacts by substituting one variable at a time against the aggregate mean markers derived from the associated baseline training collection.

      Parameters:
      isolationForest - The active IsolationForest dto context evaluating scores.
      record - An array containing the primitive parameters of the specific data row under review.
      trainingData - The collection of historical baseline records matching the active dto's state.
      Returns:
      A Map pairing string feature labels to their computed impact contribution weight percentages.

    • calculateMeans

      protected abstract double[] calculateMeans(List<T> data)
      Computes the mathematical mean values across all financial data dimensions from a collection of data records to form a centralized operational reference point.
      Parameters:
      data - The baseline source list of generic data structures.
      Returns:
      An array tracking sequentially the calculated average for the specific mapped dimensions.

    • parseData

      protected abstract double[][] parseData(List<T> data)
      Transforms a generic collection list of financial domain record objects into a primitive 2D matrix structure format compatible with the underlying mathematical library matrices.
      Parameters:
      data - The working List containing generic time-series instances.
      Returns:
      A primitive 2D double matrix tracking dimensional mapped values.
